target.title: feat(feeds): add read-only feed discovery
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a bundled `feeds` extension for curated skill/plugin catalog sources and read-only discovery.

This PR establishes the primitive without install enforcement:

- Adds `plugins.entries.feeds.config.sources[]` for configured feed documents.
- Adds feed source Doctor/health validation for ids, URLs, pinning, duplicate ids, and malformed config.
- Adds `openclaw feeds sources`, `feeds list`, and `feeds search`.
- Supports local `file://` and remote `https://` feed URLs with optional pinned `sha256:<hex>` integrity.
- Surfaces install hints in read-only discovery output, but does not install or block anything.
- Adds plugin docs and plugin inventory wiring.

## Example syntax

```jsonc
{
  "plugins": {
    "entries": {
      "feeds": {
        "enabled": true,
        "config": {
          "sources": [
            {
              "id": "company-approved",
              "url": "https://feeds.example.com/openclaw/feed.json",
              "trust": "pinned",
              "integrity": "sha256:..."
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Commands

```bash
openclaw feeds sources
openclaw feeds list
openclaw feeds search calendar --type plugin
openclaw doctor --lint
```

## Notes

This is intentionally read-only. It does not add ClawHub-native feeds, background sync, automatic installation, or runtime enforcement.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
The bundled feeds plugin can be enabled from config and the real OpenClaw CLI can read configured feed sources, list entries, and search feed entries after the branch was rebased and the remote HTTPS fetch path was moved behind the SSRF guard.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04 as root, source checkout /root/src/openclaw-feeds-87824 at 57d27be40d336c62c89a5a7fe2794ddd5170558b, Node.js v24.15.0, repo source runner scripts/run-node.mjs, isolated OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/openclaw.json and OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/state. The proof feed used a local file:// source so no external service credentials were required.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Created /tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/openclaw.json with plugins.entries.feeds.enabled=true and one source: { id: "local-proof", url: "file:///tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/feed.json", trust: "unsigned" }.
2. Ran OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/state node scripts/run-node.mjs feeds sources --json.
3. Ran OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/state node scripts/run-node.mjs feeds list --json.
4. Ran OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/state node scripts/run-node.mjs feeds search proof --type plugin --json.

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "sources": [
    {
      "id": "local-proof",
      "url": "file:///tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/feed.json",
      "enabled": true,
      "trust": "unsigned"
    }
  ]
}
{
  "entries": [
    {
      "type": "skill",
      "id": "proof-skill",
      "name": "Proof Skill",
      "description": "Local proof skill feed entry",
      "tags": ["proof", "feeds"],
      "sourceId": "local-proof",
      "feedId": "proof-feed"
    },
    {
      "type": "plugin",
      "id": "proof-plugin",
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "name": "Proof Plugin",
      "description": "Local proof plugin feed entry",
      "tags": ["proof", "plugins"],
      "sourceId": "local-proof",
      "feedId": "proof-feed"
    }
  ]
}
{
  "entries": [
    {
      "type": "plugin",
      "id": "proof-plugin",
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "name": "Proof Plugin",
      "description": "Local proof plugin feed entry",
      "tags": ["proof", "plugins"],
      "sourceId": "local-proof",
      "feedId": "proof-feed"
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix:
All three real CLI commands exited 0. The CLI loaded the enabled feeds plugin from the isolated config, reported the configured source, listed both feed entries, and filtered search results to the matching plugin entry.

What was not tested:
No external HTTPS feed endpoint was contacted in the real CLI proof. The HTTPS path is covered by the focused CLI test added in this branch, which asserts default HTTPS loads call fetchWithSsrFGuard with auditContext "feeds.feed-document" and report SSRF guard blocks to the operator.
## Validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts src/flows/bundled-health-checks.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs --noEmit
pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/feeds/src/cli.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/feed-document.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/feeds.md docs/plugins/reference.md
git diff --check
node scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs --check
```

Focused stack proof on the final condensed branch: 243 tests passed.
Latest cap-fix validation on head `57d27be40d336c62c89a5a7fe2794ddd5170558b`:

```bash
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.test.ts src/flows/bundled-health-checks.test.ts --reporter=dot
pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json extensions/feeds/src/feed-document.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/feeds/src/feed-document.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts && git diff --check
pnpm tsgo:extensions
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/state node scripts/run-node.mjs feeds sources --json
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/state node scripts/run-node.mjs feeds list --json
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof-87824/state node scripts/run-node.mjs feeds search proof --type plugin --json
```

## Feed PR stack

1. openclaw/openclaw#87824 - read-only feed discovery
2. openclaw/openclaw#87825 - approved feed installs
3. openclaw/openclaw#87826 - feed catalog policy conformance
4. openclaw/openclaw#87827 - feed lifecycle tooling
5. openclaw/openclaw#88732 - native feed search defaults and policy checks
6. openclaw/clawhub#2460 - ClawHub root feed lanes

The stack keeps OpenClaw as a feed consumer. ClawHub root feeds are producer infrastructure; enterprise or tenant feeds can be produced elsewhere using the same schema.

RFC draft: https://github.com/giodl73-repo/rfcs/blob/feeds-rfc-draft/rfcs/0004-feeds.md

target.title: feat(plugins): add Control UI entry points
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Plugins can expose gateway-authenticated HTTP routes, but there is no generic way for a plugin to register a first-class Control UI navigation entry.
- Why it matters: Plugin UIs currently require bespoke Control UI wiring, which makes real plugin UI surfaces harder to build, test, and keep out of core.
- What changed: Added generic Plugin UI Entry Points across the plugin registry, gateway protocol, launch-token plumbing, and Control UI navigation/rendering.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not add the Session Search plugin or any plugin-specific UI. A separate proof plugin lives on [`Ittiz/openclaw@forge/session-search-plugin`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/tree/forge/session-search-plugin).

## Working Example Plugin

The working example is the Session Search plugin on the plugin-only proof branch:

- Branch: [`Ittiz/openclaw@forge/session-search-plugin`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/tree/forge/session-search-plugin)
- README: [`README.md`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/README.md)
- Entry point registration: [`index.ts`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/index.ts)
- Manifest: [`openclaw.plugin.json`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/openclaw.plugin.json)

That branch intentionally contains only the plugin files, not the OpenClaw source tree. It demonstrates a real app-nav Control UI plugin that searches, inspects, injects, and resumes prior sessions through the entry-point API added by this PR.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Current-head dispatch/proof refresh: `4e36e35e2205382b8ee73df4ed0ef80bf53435e8` fixes the ClawSweeper finding that `plugins.uiEntryPoints` and `plugins.uiEntryPointLaunch` were defined but not registered in `coreGatewayHandlers`.
- Exact commands run after the dispatch fix:

```bash

corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/server-methods.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts src/agents/tool-display-config.ts
node --import tsx scripts/tool-display.ts --check
corepack pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts
corepack pnpm test src/gateway/method-scopes.test.ts
corepack pnpm build
```

- Observed result after the dispatch fix: The new regression test invokes `plugins.uiEntryPoints` and `plugins.uiEntryPointLaunch` through `coreGatewayHandlers`, proving the real gateway dispatch map now reaches the plugin-host hook handlers instead of falling through to unknown-method handling. The build gate also passes on the fixed head.

- Behavior or issue addressed: A plugin can register a Control UI app-nav entry, have it listed by the gateway, launch it through a short-lived token, and render it in Control UI without hardcoded plugin UI code. Current head tested: `87e108f70eb4eb2b7b5c540b9a574bebe0845c84`.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout in the PR worktree after merging latest upstream main, plus the running OpenClaw development gateway on `127.0.0.1:18789` with the real Session Search plugin registered as a Control UI app-nav entry. - Observed result after fix: The Control UI app navigation displayed the real Session Search plugin entry, clicking it launched a sandboxed iframe through `plugins.uiEntryPointLaunch`, the iframe rendered the plugin title/description inside Control UI, and unauthenticated direct route access stayed blocked with `401 Unauthorized`. The branch is also merged with latest upstream main, the missing UI spawn helper exports are restored while keeping Corepack fallback behavior, the host-hook fixture registers the new entry-point seam, and generated i18n metadata is clean.
- What was not tested: I did not rerun the full hosted GitHub matrix locally; GitHub CI is responsible for the complete shard set. The live browser proof used an existing development gateway and plugin instance rather than starting a separate throwaway gateway.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A.

- Root cause: N/A.
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts`
  - `ui/src/ui/navigation.test.ts`
  - `ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts`

- Scenario the test should lock in: Plugin UI entry-point registration, gateway listing, launch-token validation, plugin HTTP auth behavior, and Control UI navigation.

- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Plugins can now add authenticated Control UI navigation entries through the plugin API. No default UI changes are visible unless a plugin registers an entry point.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[plugin registers route] -> [no generic Control UI nav entry]

After:
[plugin registers entry point]
  -> [gateway lists entry point]
  -> [Control UI renders navigation item]

```

## Security Impact (required)

- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - Plugins can expose Control UI entry points, so entry points are scoped and served through gateway-authenticated plugin routes.
  - Launch uses short-lived plugin UI entry tokens instead of unauthenticated direct access.
  - Entry points declare required operator scopes.
  - Tests cover unauthorized access rejection and launch-token validation.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Runtime/container: Local source checkout, Node.js, pnpm/corepack

### Steps

2. Register a plugin with `registerControlUiEntryPoint`.
3. Start the gateway from the rebuilt checkout.
4. Open Control UI.
5. Confirm the plugin entry appears in app navigation.
6. Open the plugin entry.
7. Attempt direct unauthenticated access to the plugin route.

### Expected

- Registered plugin UI entry appears in Control UI navigation.
- Opening the entry launches the plugin UI through authenticated gateway plumbing.
- Direct unauthenticated route access is rejected.

### Actual

- Entry rendered in Control UI.
- Plugin UI opened through the Control UI entry.
- Direct unauthenticated plugin route returned `401`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets

- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

  - Control UI navigation rendering.
  - Real plugin proof using [`Ittiz/openclaw@forge/session-search-plugin`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/tree/forge/session-search-plugin).
- Edge cases checked:
  - Missing/invalid launch token is rejected.
  - Plugin-specific names are not hardcoded into core entry-point plumbing.
  - Direct unauthenticated plugin route access remains blocked.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Packaged release install from npm/tarball.
  - Mobile/native Control UI surfaces.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Plugins could expose confusing or unauthorized UI surfaces.
  - Mitigation: Entry points declare required scopes and launch through gateway-authenticated token plumbing.

- Risk: Core could accidentally become tailored to one proof plugin.
  - Mitigation: Core tests use neutral fixture plugin names; the real Session Search proof plugin is kept separate on [`Ittiz/openclaw@forge/session-search-plugin`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/tree/forge/session-search-plugin).

target.title: [AI-assisted] Add session history family lookup
target.body:
## Problem

When a threaded conversation has already been split across reset transcripts, `chat.history` and the `sessions_history` tool only read the currently active transcript. The session store can already retain the family through `usageFamilySessionIds`, but history readers do not use that chain and do not discover archived `*.jsonl.reset.<timestamp>` topic transcripts.

That means an agent/tool can be looking at the correct logical Telegram/Slack/Discord thread and still miss earlier turns after session rollover.

## Fix

Add an opt-in family history path:

- `chat.history` accepts `includeFamily: true`.
- `sessions_history` accepts `includeFamily`.
- `sessions_history` also accepts `resetAncestors` as a tool-facing alias.
- Gateway and embedded `chat.history` now share the bounded/header-checked reset archive resolver instead of using separate prefix scans.
- The shared resolver can include owned topic-suffixed reset archives such as `<sessionId>-topic-456.jsonl.reset.<timestamp>` only when the archive header matches the requested session id.
- Same-prefix colliding topic archives and headerless topic archives are ignored.
- Family read targets are capped before transcript reads, and normal `chat.history` behavior remains opt-in: UI startup does not load ancestor history.

## Validation

Current head: `a12e1bbcf082723f920c3434f3395f2861b67f89`.

- `CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts`
  - gateway shard: 84 tests passed
  - agents shard: 11 tests passed
- `CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts`
  - 36 tests passed
- `CI=true node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check`
  - `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).`
- `CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - passed
- `CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - passed
- `git diff --check`
  - passed, no output

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A logical thread whose active session has `usageFamilySessionIds` should be able to fetch earlier reset transcript turns when a caller explicitly requests family history, including owned topic-suffixed reset archives, without reading colliding or headerless topic archives. The current active transcript must also be preserved when a retained reset family has more entries than the bounded 32 read targets.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch at current head `a12e1bbcf0`, using temporary on-disk session directories, the real Gateway `chat.history` RPC integration harness, embedded-mode `chat.history`, and the same reset archive filename/header patterns that OpenClaw creates for topic transcripts.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. Created temporary session directories and session-store entries with `usageFamilySessionIds` linking an ancestor session and the current session.
  2. Wrote an owned plain ancestor reset archive and an owned topic-suffixed ancestor archive with matching `type: "session"` headers.
  3. Wrote newer same-prefix colliding and headerless topic reset archives in the same sessions directory.
  4. Invoked the real Gateway `chat.history` RPC path with and without `includeFamily: true`.
  5. Created a second Gateway/RPC case with 40 ancestor session ids before the current id in `usageFamilySessionIds`.
  6. Invoked embedded-mode `chat.history(includeFamily)` through the sessions tool stub, including the same >32-family-target cap case.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from current head:
  ```text
  $ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts

  Tests  11 passed (11)
  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 44.69s

  $ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts

  Tests  36 passed (36)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 30.16s

  # passed

  # passed

  $ git diff --check
  # no output
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** Default `chat.history` excludes ancestor reset archive content, while `chat.history(includeFamily)` includes the owned plain ancestor archive, includes the owned topic-suffixed ancestor archive, includes current-session content, and rejects the colliding/headerless topic reset archives. In the >32-target Gateway/RPC regression, `chat.history(includeFamily)` contains `current survives family target cap`, contains early ancestor content, and does not read the ancestor entries beyond the target cap. Embedded `chat.history(includeFamily)` now reads the current active transcript first, then current archives, then ancestor archives/active transcripts; its >32-target regression also keeps the current transcript and drops over-cap ancestors.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live Telegram topic end-to-end, and this PR does not add prompt-level carryover summaries or automatic prompt injection. It only adds explicit family history retrieval for gateway/tool callers.

## Current head addendum: `8c164eeaa3`

ClawSweeper's `a12e1bbcf0` review finding is addressed in this head:

- Family target selection still reserves the current active transcript before the 32-target cap is reached.
- Family output order is now ancestor transcripts first, then current-session reset archives, then the current active transcript, so final `limit` trimming keeps the active session tail.
- Embedded `chat.history(includeFamily)` mirrors the Gateway order.
- Added over-limit regressions proving `includeFamily` with a small final limit keeps current active history.

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, trim entries, drop blank/whitespace-only entries, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Normalize Gateway `agent.run` `toolsAllow` before dispatching to the runner, matching the `sessions_spawn` normalization behavior.
- Keep CLI-backed runtime handling aligned with current `main`: runtime `toolsAllow` is forwarded to the CLI harness so the harness can fail closed when it cannot enforce the allowlist.

## Current upstream status
Latest `upstream/main` already includes broad subagent/runtime work and embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support. This PR remains scoped to the missing public/control-plane plumbing for `sessions_spawn.toolsAllow`, plus Gateway normalization for raw `agent.run` inputs.

## Change Type
- [x] Feature

## Scope
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the policy is applied to the spawned child embedded runtime so the child receives only allowed tool schemas. Gateway `agent.run` also normalizes direct `toolsAllow` input before runner dispatch.

Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout at current PR head `230b54baa3175fafeb7160922f510b489a6d1887`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, local Gateway on loopback token auth with `gateway.mode=local`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json` through Gateway (not `--local`), and a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server that records every `/v1/responses` request tool schema.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started a local mock OpenAI Responses server from `/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-head-230b54ba.LskIfs/mock-openai-toolsallow.cjs`, started OpenClaw Gateway from the same checkout, then ran the parent agent through Gateway:

```text
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-head-230b54ba.LskIfs/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-head-230b54ba.LskIfs/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=<loopback-proof-token> OPENAI_API_KEY=mock node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json --session-key agent:default:tools-allow-parent --message 'SUBAGENT_TOOLS_ALLOW_PARENT_PROOF: call sessions_spawn exactly once with toolsAllow ["read"].' --timeout 90
```

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the current-head Gateway proof summarizer. The parent initial request had `sessions_spawn`; the spawned child request had only `read`, with `exec`, `sessions_spawn`, and `sessions_yield` absent. The mock log had 3 requests total: parent initial tool call, parent follow-up after child result, and the restricted spawned child request.

```json
{
  "head": "230b54baa3175fafeb7160922f510b489a6d1887",
  "gatewayAgentRunStatus": "ok",
  "gatewayAgentRunId": "8843fa2b-8a41-49d6-aaa4-ae3487155aa5",
  "finalAssistantVisibleText": "CHILD_DONE",
  "parentTools": [
    "exec",
    "read",
    "sessions_spawn",
    "sessions_yield",
    "subagents"
  ],
  "restrictedChildTools": [
    "read"
  ],
  "restrictedChildHasRead": true,
  "restrictedChildHasExec": false,
  "restrictedChildHasSessionsSpawn": false,
  "restrictedChildHasSessionsYield": false,
  "totalMockRequests": 3,
  "proofDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-head-230b54ba.LskIfs"
}
```

Additional current-head verification already run after resolving conflicts:

```text
node scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
prompt snapshot check passed

node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
oxlint shards passed

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts --testNamePattern 'toolsAllow|timestamp context|canonical Anthropic' --reporter=dot
focused toolsAllow vitest shards passed
```

GitHub checks observed on current head: PR is mergeable/clean and the current `Real behavior proof` workflow check is passing.

Observed result after fix: A real Gateway-backed parent agent run called `sessions_spawn` with `toolsAllow:["read"]`, the child run completed with `CHILD_DONE`, and the actual spawned child model request only advertised `read`. Disallowed parent/session/runtime tools were not present in the spawned child request.

What was not tested: A paid external provider was not used. The proof used a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server to avoid provider cost while exercising the real Gateway, real embedded runner, real `sessions_spawn` tool call, and real child model request construction.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`.
- Scenario locked in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported ACP runtime, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, is inherited by persisted child sessions, and is forwarded to CLI harnesses for fail-closed handling.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. `runtime="acp"` rejects `toolsAllow` clearly because ACP cannot enforce this embedded-runner allowlist.

## Security Impact
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

Risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native subagent runs. Unsupported ACP paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced. CLI-backed runs receive the runtime allowlist at the harness boundary so the harness can fail closed when enforcement is unavailable.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect all CLI harnesses to enforce runtime allowlists.
  - Mitigation: OpenClaw forwards the allowlist to the CLI harness boundary for fail-closed handling instead of silently dropping it.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native child tools with `toolsAllow`; the spawned child embedded model request advertises only the allowed tools instead of inheriting the full parent native tool set.
- Real environment tested: macOS local checkout at PR head `99c0bdd3cb556a7a407c0ae45e0931fa82617dc3`; temporary OpenClaw state/config; real Gateway loopback token auth; real `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run`; real `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json`; deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server logging `/v1/responses` requests.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a repo-local Node proof harness that created temp state/config, started the mock OpenAI-compatible server, started the real Gateway, then invoked `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json --session-key agent:default:tools-allow-parent --message 'SUBAGENT_TOOLS_ALLOW_PARENT_PROOF: call sessions_spawn exactly once with toolsAllow ["read"].' --timeout 120` with the temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.
- Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "head": "99c0bdd3cb556a7a407c0ae45e0931fa82617dc3",
  "gatewayAgentExitCode": 0,
  "gatewayAgentRunStatus": "ok",
  "finalAssistantVisibleText": "CHILD_DONE",
  "stopReason": "stop",
  "toolSummary": {
    "calls": 1,
    "tools": ["sessions_spawn"],
    "failures": 0
  },
  "parentTools": ["agents_list", "apply_patch", "browser", "canvas", "create_goal", "cron", "dir_fetch", "dir_list", "edit", "exec", "file_fetch", "file_write", "gateway", "get_goal", "image", "image_generate", "memory_get", "memory_search", "message", "music_generate", "nodes", "pdf", "process", "qqbot_remind", "read", "session_status", "sessions_history", "sessions_list", "sessions_send", "sessions_spawn", "sessions_yield", "skill_workshop", "subagents", "tts", "update_goal", "update_plan", "video_generate", "web_fetch", "web_search", "write"],
  "restrictedChildTools": ["read"],
  "restrictedChildHasRead": true,
  "restrictedChildHasExec": false,
  "restrictedChildHasSessionsSpawn": false,
  "restrictedChildHasSessionsYield": false,
  "totalMockRequests": 3,
  "proofDir": "/var/folders/b_/fvf7_fqd4y77y63pd76x8gb80000gn/T/openclaw-78441-proof-head-99c0bdd3-J7y7zd"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The parent request still exposes the full native tool set and calls `sessions_spawn` once, while the spawned child request exposes only `["read"]`; it has `read` and does not have `exec`, `sessions_spawn`, or `sessions_yield`. The real Gateway agent run exits 0 with status `ok`.
- What was not tested: A paid external provider. The proof used a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock to avoid cost while still exercising the real Gateway, embedded runner, `sessions_spawn`, and child model request construction.

target.title: [codex] Further optimize Telegram media and reply context flow
target.body:
## Summary

- add scoped indexes to the Telegram message cache so `recentBefore` and `around` avoid scanning and sorting the full cache
- cache successful Telegram media resolutions by file identity and relevant runtime options
- revalidate cached media paths before reuse so TTL cleanup cannot leave stale attachments behind
- add regression coverage for thread-scoped chat windows, media cache hits, and stale media cache eviction
- bump `@openclaw/fs-safe` to 0.2.1 so focused Telegram tests can install and resolve `@openclaw/fs-safe/config` cleanly

## Context

This builds on earlier Telegram-route performance work by `obviyus`, further optimizing the same flow by tightening reply-context lookups and avoiding repeated Telegram media hydration.

## Why

Telegram reply-context building can repeatedly query recent chat windows and hydrate the same reply media. This PR keeps those hot paths bounded while preserving the existing chat-wide and thread-specific behavior.

The media cache also checks the saved file still exists before returning a cached path, because long-running gateways can remove saved media through TTL cleanup.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram reply-context lookups stay thread-scoped or chat-wide as requested, repeated Telegram media hydration reuses the cached saved path, and a deleted saved media file is evicted and re-downloaded instead of returning a stale path.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway from this PR head (`b8ad83191132ea2cdb1c9a40312ded58f58444ba`) on macOS, running `OpenClaw 2026.5.10-beta.1` with the Telegram plugin loaded from the PR checkout. Telegram ingress used the real Bot API webhook for `@acecoderbot` through a temporary Cloudflare tunnel. The LLM provider was mocked only to make the response deterministic; Telegram update delivery, reply-context hydration, message-cache persistence, and reply dispatch all ran through the PR checkout.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `pnpm openclaw --log-level debug gateway run --port 19043 --auth token --token [redacted] --bind loopback --verbose --ws-log compact` from the PR checkout with an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, a real Telegram Bot API webhook, and a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible provider. Sent `PR80243 live seed OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_1` to `@acecoderbot`, then sent a real Telegram reply to that DM with `PR80243 live reply-context OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_2`. Also ran a local media/cache proof through `node --import tsx` using a real temporary state directory and local trusted Bot API file path.
- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted live runtime logs and persisted cache/model-input excerpts from the actual PR gateway run:


- **Observed result after fix:** The live Telegram reply reached OpenClaw with `reply_to_message`, OpenClaw logged `telegram reply-context` for `replyToId=10034`, the inbound prompt included `has_reply_context: true`, the nearby reply target window included both the original DM and the reply, and both Telegram messages completed through the PR gateway. The supplemental cache/media proof showed thread-scoped windows stayed scoped, chat-wide windows included cross-topic history, the second media resolution reused the cached path without another `getFile`, and stale saved-path eviction forced a fresh `getFile`.
- **What was not tested:** No additional gaps for the live Telegram reply-context behavior. The media cache proof used OpenClaw's real resolver and media store with a local trusted Bot API file path rather than downloading a large external Telegram media file during this proof run.

target.title: fix(telegram): honor ingest config before skipping unaddressed group media
target.body:
## Summary

When a Telegram group/topic has `ingest: true` configured, media messages (photos, videos, documents) that don't @mention the bot should still be processed by the ingest hook. Currently, `shouldSkipMediaDownloadForUnaddressedMentionGroup` returns `true` (skip media download) **before** the ingest config is consulted — so media-bearing messages are silently dropped for ingest-enabled groups, while text messages flow through correctly.

This is the media-path counterpart to the text-message ingest behavior already working in `bot-message-context.body.ts:438-442`.

## Fix

Insert an ingest opt-in check before the `mentionDecision.shouldSkip` guard in `shouldSkipMediaDownloadForUnaddressedMentionGroup`. If `topicConfig?.ingest ?? groupConfig?.ingest` is `true`, return `false` (don't skip) — matching the existing resolution order used for text messages.

**1 file changed, 7 insertions(+)**

## How to Test

1. Configure a Telegram supergroup with `requireMention: true` and `ingest: true`
2. Register a `message:received` plugin hook
3. Send a photo without text and without @mentioning the bot
4. Before this patch: hook never fires, log shows "skipping group media before download"
5. After this patch: hook fires with the media message

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `shouldSkipMediaDownloadForUnaddressedMentionGroup` now checks `topicConfig?.ingest ?? groupConfig?.ingest` before returning true for unaddressed group media, so ingest-enabled groups receive media messages through the ingest hook path.
- **Environment tested:** macOS, Node v25.8.2, pnpm build of openclaw/openclaw at 330545f3
- **Steps run after the patch:** `pnpm build` completed in 84s. Ran the verification suite for the telegram extension module — 4 tests in `bot-handlers.runtime.test.ts` and 82 tests in `bot.test.ts` all pass. Verified the bundled dist output contains the ingest guard.
- **Evidence after fix:**
```
$ grep -n "ingest" extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts
889:    // Honor explicit ingest opt-in: ingest-enabled groups/topics should not
891:    const ingestEnabled = topicConfig?.ingest ?? groupConfig?.ingest;
892:    if (ingestEnabled === true) {

$ node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const c=fs.readFileSync('dist/bot-DSeNTaDb.js','utf8'); const idx=c.indexOf('shouldSkipMediaDownload'); const s=c.substring(idx,idx+300); console.log(s.includes('ingest') ? 'PASS: ingest guard present in dist' : 'FAIL');"
PASS: ingest guard present in dist
```
- **Observed result after fix:** The ingest guard compiles cleanly, passes all existing telegram tests (4 + 82), and is present in the bundled dist output. The fix follows the identical `topicConfig?.ingest ?? groupConfig?.ingest` resolution pattern already used in `bot-message-context.body.ts:438-441` for text messages.
- **What was not tested:** Live Telegram supergroup media delivery (requires a running Telegram bot with forum topics and a plugin hook). The fix is a bounded guard addition that mirrors an existing proven pattern — the text-message ingest path has been in production since the feature shipped.

target.title: feat(feeds): add native feed search defaults
target.body:
## Summary

Adds the opt-in path for native `openclaw skills search` and `openclaw plugins search` to use configured catalog feeds.

This PR keeps the rollout explicit:

- `--catalog-feeds` searches configured feed sources for one command.
- `--feed-source <id>` narrows native search to specific configured source ids.
- `plugins.entries.feeds.config.search.default: true` makes native skill/plugin search use feeds by default.
- `plugins.entries.feeds.config.search.sources` optionally narrows the default feed source set; omitted means all enabled sources, and an explicit empty list means no sources.
- Policy can require native feed search defaults with `feeds.search.requireDefault` and `feeds.search.requireSources`.

## Policy syntax

```jsonc
{
  "feeds": {
    "search": {
      "requireDefault": true,
      "requireSources": ["company-approved"],
    },
  },
}
```

## Config syntax

```jsonc
{
  "plugins": {
    "entries": {
      "feeds": {
        "enabled": true,
        "config": {
          "search": {
            "default": true,
            "sources": ["company-approved"],
          },
          "sources": [
            {
              "id": "company-approved",
              "url": "https://feeds.example.com/company.json",
              "trust": "pinned",
              "integrity": "sha256:...",
            },
          ],
        },
      },
    },
  },
}
```

## Stack

1. #87824 - read-only feed discovery
2. #87825 - approved feed installs
3. #87826 - feed catalog policy conformance
4. #87827 - feed lifecycle tooling
5. This PR - native search defaults and policy checks

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Native `openclaw skills search` and `openclaw plugins search` can search configured catalog feeds explicitly with `--catalog-feeds` / `--feed-source`, and use feeds by default when the Feeds plugin search default is enabled.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04, source checkout `/root/src/openclaw-feeds-88732` at `c487721eafb27b0226e3cdfed5a54d8ddef2d12f`, source CLI via `node scripts/run-node.mjs`, temporary OpenClaw config/state/home, and one pinned local `file://` feed source. No ClawHub or external network service was contacted by the proof search commands.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/feed-search-options.test.ts src/cli/plugins-search-command.test.ts src/cli/skills-cli.commands.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts src/flows/bundled-health-checks.test.ts --reporter=dot`
2. `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json src/cli/feed-search-options.ts src/cli/feed-search-options.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.ts src/cli/plugins-search-command.ts src/cli/plugins-search-command.test.ts src/cli/skills-cli.ts src/cli/skills-cli.commands.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts`
3. `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/cli/plugins.md docs/cli/skills.md docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/feeds.md src/cli/feed-search-options.ts src/cli/feed-search-options.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.ts src/cli/plugins-search-command.ts src/cli/plugins-search-command.test.ts src/cli/skills-cli.ts src/cli/skills-cli.commands.test.ts extensions/feeds/api.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts && git diff --check`
4. `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
5. `pnpm tsgo:core`
6. `node scripts/run-node.mjs skills search spreadsheet --catalog-feeds --feed-source approved --json`
7. `node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins search calendar --catalog-feeds --feed-source approved --json`
8. `node scripts/run-node.mjs skills search spreadsheet --json`
9. `node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins search calendar --json`

Evidence after fix:
Validation passed: focused Vitest passed 3 shards with 8, 64, and 371 tests; oxlint passed with 0 warnings/errors; scoped oxfmt plus `git diff --check` passed; `pnpm tsgo:extensions` passed; `pnpm tsgo:core` passed.

Source CLI proof transcript:

```text
PROOF_ROOT=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-88732-proof.sEIwUD
FEED_INTEGRITY=sha256:478b4a157e66822becaf8884665aeba82e4c50cca495d0ddafb7e1c30cee321e

## skills explicit
{
  "results": [
    {
      "type": "skill",
      "id": "excel-review",
      "version": "1.2.0",
      "name": "Excel Review",
      "description": "Review spreadsheets",
      "sourceId": "approved",
      "feedId": "company-approved"
    }
  ]
}

## plugins explicit
{
  "results": [
    {
      "type": "plugin",
      "id": "calendar-helper",
      "version": "0.3.0",
      "name": "Calendar Helper",
      "description": "Calendar automation",
      "sourceId": "approved",
      "feedId": "company-approved"
    }
  ]
}

## skills default
{
  "results": [
    {
      "type": "skill",
      "id": "excel-review",
      "sourceId": "approved",
      "feedId": "company-approved"
    }
  ]
}

## plugins default
{
  "results": [
    {
      "type": "plugin",
      "id": "calendar-helper",
      "sourceId": "approved",
      "feedId": "company-approved"
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix:
Explicit feed-backed `skills search` returned only the feed skill entry, explicit feed-backed `plugins search` returned only the feed plugin entry, and the same two commands used the configured feed by default when `plugins.entries.feeds.config.search.default` was true with `sources: ["approved"]`.

What was not tested:
No live external HTTPS feed endpoint or ClawHub service was contacted in the source CLI proof; the proof uses an isolated pinned local `file://` feed, and HTTPS feed loading is covered by the lower feed stack tests.

## Validation

- `codex review --commit HEAD`
- `node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts src/cli/feed-search-options.test.ts src/cli/plugins-search-command.test.ts src/cli/skills-cli.commands.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node node_modules/oxlint/bin/oxlint extensions/feeds/api.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts src/cli/feed-search-options.ts src/cli/feed-search-options.test.ts src/cli/plugins-search-command.test.ts src/cli/skills-cli.commands.test.ts extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts --tsconfig tsconfig.json`
- `git diff --check`

## Feed PR stack

1. openclaw/openclaw#87824 - read-only feed discovery
2. openclaw/openclaw#87825 - approved feed installs
3. openclaw/openclaw#87826 - feed catalog policy conformance
4. openclaw/openclaw#87827 - feed lifecycle tooling
5. openclaw/openclaw#88732 - native feed search defaults and policy checks
6. openclaw/clawhub#2460 - ClawHub root feed lanes

The stack keeps OpenClaw as a feed consumer. ClawHub root feeds are producer infrastructure; enterprise or tenant feeds can be produced elsewhere using the same schema.

RFC draft: https://github.com/giodl73-repo/rfcs/blob/feeds-rfc-draft/rfcs/0004-feeds.md

target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: open, download, and copy
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably
- Binds paired Control UI read-token media fetches to the requested session key before serving managed image bytes
- Renders generated image frames with the same rounded clipping as other chat media
- Pre-scales known generated-image dimensions so the frame matches the visible thumbnail/image size
- Reserves pending preview space while managed image blobs load, preventing chat scroll/window jumps
- Serves timeline images from configurable thumbnail routes while keeping open/download/copy on the full image
- Adds `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` for local/managed Control UI thumbnails
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract

## Why

Generated images were being displayed without the full media affordances we expect in chat. The implementation also needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry so the new actions attach to the visible image, rounded corners clip the actual image, and the chat viewport does not jump while a managed preview is still loading.

The follow-up fixes make the preview/full-image split explicit: right-click View Image uses the thumbnail preview, while click/Open/Download/Copy use the original full-size image.

## Testing

- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm config:schema:check`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 900s pnpm test test/vitest/vitest.acp.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.shared-core.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.tasks.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.utils.config.ts`
- Browser proof: temporary Vite page rendered the actual Control UI chat image component in system Chromium via Playwright, then clicked Open, Download, and Copy.

## Branch Notes

This branch is based directly on latest `upstream/main` and intentionally does not include PR #75727 gateway changes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Generated/managed chat images are served through authenticated thumbnail previews and full-size Open/Download/Copy targets. Current head: `9f63b88296432c0d775eff026b0bea3eb40a500b`; behavior proof below was captured at `1f608477f2d9bfaadcac24bef246d766061b7192`, and later heads only change the CI Node fallback script.
- Real environment tested: Local PR checkout on Linux, focused Gateway test shard, Vite-served Control UI module, system Chromium `/usr/bin/chromium` through Playwright with `--no-sandbox`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the managed-image Gateway test shard, then served a temporary Control UI proof page with Vite, rendered `renderMessageGroup` from `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts`, waited for the managed image blob preview, hovered the image frame, clicked Open/Download/Copy, and captured the browser-evaluated fetch/action state.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
$ PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts
```

```json

  "result": {
```

- Observed result after fix: After merging latest upstream main, the prior current-head `checks-node-core-runtime-shared` timeout was fixed by pinning the legacy cron model-override harness to the embedded-agent mock path, the changelog conflict was resolved, the current-main Unreleased entries were restored again against `bf95f762b5df521a2e5846073793f95cb42deb8b` then the release-owned changelog entry was removed and gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide was documented; the merged verification bundle passed locally before the docs-only cleanup. The browser rendered the managed generated image from a blob thumbnail preview, displayed the Open/Download/Copy controls, fetched the thumbnail for preview, fetched the full route for actions with the session-bound read token headers, opened a blob URL, prepared a PNG download, and performed one clipboard image write.
Media auth repair proof after preserving owner/admin authorization for managed image bytes:

```text
$ git diff --check && corepack pnpm check:changelog-attributions
$ node scripts/check-changelog-attributions.mjs
passed
```

target.title: fix(test): harden script probe bounds
target.body:
Summary:
- harden the kitchen-sink RPC POSIX process sampler so direct gateway roots are sampled without overriding the existing gateway-title child fallback
- reject unsafe decimal `content-length` values before reading kitchen-sink RPC probe bodies
- reject unsafe avatar probe `content-length` values before reading Clawtributors avatar bodies

Verification:
- `node --check scripts/e2e/kitchen-sink-rpc-walk.mjs`
- `node --check --experimental-strip-types scripts/update-clawtributors.ts`
- focused direct Node repros for the POSIX root sampler, gateway-title fallback, and unsafe kitchen-sink `content-length`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` (clean)

target.title: fix: recover stuck Control UI chat runs
target.body:
## Summary
- Repairs #42258 as the canonical fix path for #42213.
- Keeps the stuck-run recovery scoped to the Control UI chat lifecycle and queued-message flush behavior.
- Addresses hydrated Greptile findings: do not surface transient background probe failures as user-visible errors, avoid double watchdog scheduling, and remove redundant run-activity writes.

## Credit
This continues the contributor work from @siewcapital in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/42258. ClawSweeper should preserve that source attribution when updating the branch.

## Verification
- pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts
- pnpm check:changed
- Real behavior proof should cover a Control UI chat run that misses its terminal event, recovers idle state via the watchdog/session proof path, reloads history, and flushes a queued follow-up without showing a transient probe error banner.

ClawSweeper 🐠 replacement reef notes:
- Cluster: gitcrawl-1599-fix-register-abortcontroller-for-agent-rpc-runs-so-chat-abort-wo
- Source PRs: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/42258
- Credit: Repair contributor branch for source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/42258 by @siewcapital; preserve source PR attribution in the repaired PR history/body.; Do not edit CHANGELOG.md for normal OpenClaw repair work; keep release-note context in the PR body and eventual squash message.; Agent RPC abort support is already credited/landed via https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/71214 and should not be reopened in this repair.
- Validation: pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts; pnpm check:changed
- Replacement reason: ClawSweeper could not update the source PR branch directly, so it opened a writable replacement PR instead.
- Repair fallback: GitHub rejected the repair branch push because it updates workflow files and the ClawSweeper app token does not have workflows permission

Inherited issue-closing references from the source PR:
Closes #42213

Co-author credit kept:
- @siewcapital: Co-authored-by: Siew's Capital Jarvis <264786654+siewcapital@users.noreply.github.com>

fish notes: model gpt-5.5, reasoning high; reviewed against ae868b7b18df.

target.title: feat: channel echo / session pinning
target.body: ## Pin-from-here: channel-agnostic turn mirroring A session can mirror its turns to other threads bound to the **same** session. You run `/pin on` in a thread; from then on, when any other thread of that session drives a turn, this thread receives it — rendered **natively through its own channel's dispatch**, persisted as that channel's own turn. ### Authorization (the rework) The original revision let an operator add an echo target to an arbitrary chat id; the maintainer flagged that as streaming session content to any chat. This revision removes that surface entirely: - **`/pin`** is in-chat, opt-in, and binds only the session already bound to *this* thread — every mirror recipient is, by construction, an already-authorized participant of that session. There is no "stream to an arbitrary chat id" path. - Operator echo adds require a session participant (`targetMatchesSessionParticipant`), fail-closed. ### How the mirror renders — channel-agnostic, by reuse not reimplementation The agent runs **once**; its parsed event stream is broadcast on the agent-event bus keyed by `runId`. For each pinned target, the core hands that target's **own channel dispatch** a bus-sourced `replyResolver` (`createMirrorReplyResolver`) that replays the origin run's stream instead of calling the model: - `dispatchInboundMessageWithDispatcher` already accepts a pluggable `replyResolver`. `launchMirrorDispatch` (core, channel-agnostic) drives each target through its registered mirror dispatcher with that resolver. - Telegram registers a thin `dispatchMirror` that synthesizes an inbound for the pinned chat/thread and runs its **existing** `dispatchTelegramMessage` with the resolver. So drafts, streaming, formatting, and persistence all follow **that account's own config** — streaming-capable + on → the mirror streams; off → final only. Nothing is forced; nothing is reimplemented per channel. - **Loop-safe by construction:** the `replyResolver` bypasses the agent-run path, so a mirror turn never re-enters the mirror fan-out. The post-hoc final echo skips any target a mirror turn rendered (handled-mark dedup) so there's no double delivery. This replaces the earlier per-channel streaming-echo *renderer* (telegram-only, reimplemented rendering) with one small channel-agnostic mechanism that reuses each channel's pipeline. **Net −894 LOC**: deletes `echo-renderer*`, `echo-streaming`, `streaming-progress-callbacks`, the `registerEchoRendererFactory` plugin-api plumbing, and the `plugin-sdk/channel-echo` subpath; adds a small core launcher/registry (reusing the existing `echo-mirror-resolver`) plus a thin telegram adapter. ### Real behavior proof (Telegram-desktop, mantis-equivalent) - **Behavior or issue addressed**: Pin-from-here mirrors a session turn into pinned target threads by re-running each target channel's own dispatch with a bus-sourced reply resolver, so the mirror renders + persists natively (streaming/verbose per that channel's own config) instead of a flat post-hoc echo. - **Real environment tested**: A real OpenClaw gateway built from this branch in a Linux crabbox with a desktop; a real Telegram bot + a real Telegram account on Telegram Desktop; a real `claude-cli` backend (`sonnet-4-6`/`opus-4-8`, live Anthropic API) with exec tools; the tdlib user-driver sending real messages. This recreates the Mantis telegram-desktop proof flow locally (same mechanism: telegram-desktop + user-driver + screen capture). - **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Started the gateway from the built branch; in a Telegram thread sent `/new`, `/verbose on`, `/pin on`; then drove the same session from webchat (`chat.send`) with a tool-using prompt (`sleep 3; hostname`, and a two-step `date -u` + `uname -a` narrated before each command); screen-recorded Telegram Desktop and captured the gateway debug log. - **Evidence after fix**: Direct after-fix artifacts — mirror-render screenshot [`.proof/pin-from-here-mirror-dispatch.png`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof/pin-from-here-evidence/.proof/pin-from-here-mirror-dispatch.png), multi-account screenshot [`.proof/pin-from-here-multiaccount.png`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof/pin-from-here-evidence/.proof/pin-from-here-multiaccount.png), and the full **destination-revocation run + gateway log** [`.proof/pin-from-here-revocation.txt`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof/pin-from-here-evidence/.proof/pin-from-here-revocation.txt). The revocation log shows the mirror deliver when enabled (`messageId=0` synthetic inbound + `outbound send ok chatId=…`), then `[outbound/echo] Echo delivery suppressed … (destination disabled/revoked)` after the group is disabled. - **Observed result after fix**: (a) Enabled group: the pinned Telegram thread receives the webchat-triggered turn's real reply, rendered natively (verbose `💬`+`🛠️` per the destination's config), exactly once. (b) **Disabled (revoked) group**: zero sends to the group and the tdlib driver observing it reports `NO_NEW_MESSAGE` — neither the native mirror nor the echo fallback delivers. Confirmed on both hot-reload and gateway-restart disable, at current head `0193a3d`. - **What was not tested**: Nothing material remains unproven on current head. **Destination revocation is now proven live** (and a real gap it surfaced is fixed — see *Destination revocation* below). Multi-account routing is also proven on current head — see below. #### Multi-account routing — current head (account-aware dispatch) Two real Telegram accounts in one gateway: `default` = bot #2 in GID `-1003900553563`, `acct2` = bot #3 (`@jeeves_oc_acct2_bot`) in a separate forum group GID2 `-1004389798714` (bot #3 promoted admin; bot #2 is **not** a member of GID2). The echo target for GID2 is set with `accountId: acct2`, then a **webchat-origin** turn drives the session. Gateway debug log (`.proof/pin-from-here-multiaccount-log.txt`): ```text [telegram] [acct2] starting provider (@jeeves_oc_acct2_bot) [telegram] [default] starting provider (@JeeevesOpenClawTelegram2bot) message received: channel=webchat ... sessionKey=agent:main:telegram:group:-1004389798714:topic:1 message dispatch started: channel=telegram ... source=replyResolver ← the mirror message dispatch completed: channel=telegram ... source=replyResolver outcome=completed ``` The webchat-origin turn mirrored into GID2 (`source=replyResolver` = the bus-sourced mirror), with **zero** `mirror: no dispatcher` fail-closed warnings (the acct2 dispatcher resolved). Because **bot #2 is not in GID2** (`getChatMember` → `chat not found`), a successful mirror delivery there could *only* be the **acct2** account (bot #3) — proving the mirror routes through the target's own account, not the first-registered/default one. #### Destination revocation — live proof (and a gap it caught, now fixed) Proving this **live** (real Telegram `/pin` via the tdlib user-driver, not unit-only) surfaced a real gap the unit tests missed: the revocation gate only covered the **native mirror** (which re-runs the channel's inbound admission). The prompt / post-hoc **echo** path (`fireEchoDeliveries`) delivers through the *channel-agnostic* raw send, which has **no** admission gate — so a disabled pinned group kept receiving echoes, and when the mirror fan-out didn't fire (e.g. right after the channel restart that disabling a group triggers) the post-hoc echo leaked the conversation to the revoked destination. **Fix** (commit `083338a`): a per-channel **echo-admission predicate** (`channel-admission.ts`), registered per account exactly like the mirror dispatcher (last-wins, unregister on stop, **fail closed**). `fireEchoDeliveries` filters targets through it; telegram registers a predicate that reports group/topic-disabled from its live config. Now a disabled destination gets **neither** the native mirror **nor** the echo fallback. **Stop/reload window** (commit `8519433`): a channel that unregisters its admission predicate (account stop/reload drops the predicate *and* the dispatcher together) must not revert to admit-all. A sticky, import-cycle-free `channel-mirror-capability` marker records channels that have registered a mirror dispatcher; the echo gate then **fails closed** for a mirror-capable channel whenever no predicate resolves, so the raw echo can't leak to a revoked destination during the unregister window. Non-mirror channels admit echoes unchanged. Unit-covered (`channel-admission.test.ts`). **Telegram DM denial** (commits `219810d`, `fbd5ee2`): the same gap existed for a pinned **DM**. Three parts: (a) `enforceTelegramDmAccess` denial returned null *without* the revocation marker → `dispatchMirror` threw → unmark → raw echo could reach the denied DM; now a denied DM in a mirror context calls `onMirrorAdmissionBlocked` (keep the mark, suppress the post-hoc). (b) The synthetic mirror inbound used sender id `0`, so the DM access gate *rejected every already-authorized (paired/allowlisted) DM*; for a private chat the chat id **is** the target user's id, so it's now the synthetic sender — an authorized DM mirrors, a revoked one is denied. (c) The echo-admission predicate now re-runs **live DM authorization** (`resolveTelegramDmAllow` + `isTelegramDmAccessAllowed`) for a direct chat, so the raw echo also honors DM policy/pairing/allowlist revocation (the predicate is async; admission errors fail closed). Unit-covered: `bot-message-context.mirror.test.ts` (DM-denial → `onMirrorAdmissionBlocked`), `channel-admission.test.ts` (async predicate + fail-closed), `echo.test.ts` (async suppression). **Live-proven** (real DM: driver/Cameron DMs bot #2, `/pin on` in the DM): with `dmPolicy: open` the authorized DM mirrors (validating the sender-identity fix); after `dmPolicy: disabled` the DM gets **zero** sends, the driver observing it reports `NO_NEW_MESSAGE`, and the gateway logs `Echo delivery suppressed … telegram:8661849123 (destination disabled/revoked)` — see the *DIRECT-DM REVOCATION* section of [`.proof/pin-from-here-revocation.txt`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof/pin-from-here-evidence/.proof/pin-from-here-revocation.txt). **Registry ownership** (open maintainer decision): the mirror-dispatcher + echo-admission registries are global, caller-keyed, last-wins maps with no per-plugin ownership enforcement. They are exported from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-outbound` and **only the bundled telegram channel uses them**. Whether to keep them as a public seam, hand them out through an owner-scoped registrar, or keep them internal is a maintainer API call — flagged explicitly in `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`. (An attempt to hide them on a repo-local subpath was reverted: it fought the extension package-boundary + surface-budget guards for no real gain.) The branch is also **merged up to current `openclaw/openclaw` main** (conflicts in `send.ts` / `agent-runner-execution.ts` resolved). The plugin-SDK surface-budget guard is kept and passing; its budgets were raised to the merged-main surface actuals (the +10 entrypoints / +333 exports growth is from main's 203 commits, not this PR's ~6 exports). **Live proof** (single gateway; control then revocation; the group is a real forum supergroup, pinned via a genuine `/pin on` Telegram command): ```text # CONTROL (group ENABLED): the mirror delivers message received: channel=telegram messageId=0 ... source=dispatchInboundMessage ← native mirror [telegram] outbound send ok ... chatId=-1003900553563 messageId=404 ← lands in the group # disable the group, then drive again: [reload] config hot reload applied (channels.telegram.groups.-1003900553563.enabled) [outbound/echo] Echo delivery suppressed for telegram:telegram:-1003900553563 (destination disabled/revoked) ``` - **Before the fix:** the driver observed `📱 [via webchat] …` arriving in the disabled group (+1 send). - **After the fix:** `sends-to-GID after revoke = control count (0 added)`, and the **tdlib driver observing the group reports `NO_NEW_MESSAGE`** — the disabled destination receives nothing. Confirmed on **both** revocation paths: config hot-reload **and** a full gateway restart. Unit coverage: `channel-admission.test.ts` (registry + fail-closed) and an `echo.test.ts` suppression case. Full redacted run + gateway log for both paths: [`.proof/pin-from-here-revocation.txt`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof/pin-from-here-evidence/.proof/pin-from-here-revocation.txt). The same run filmed in Telegram Desktop — the bot rendering into GID2 is **"Jeeves OC Test Account 2" (`@jeeves_oc_acct2_bot` = acct2)**, mirroring the webchat-origin turn (`📱 [via webchat] …`) and its reply natively: Account-aware dispatch also fails closed on an **explicit account mismatch** (a target pinning a different account than the only registered one is never mirrored through the wrong account) — unit-regressed. Recreation of the Mantis telegram-desktop flow on a local patched crabbox: a real gateway built from this branch (current `openclaw/openclaw` main + this work), Telegram Desktop + the user-driver + screen capture, driven by a **real `claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6` backend with an exec tool call** (not a mock). A Telegram thread runs `/pin on`; webchat then drives the same session with a tool-using prompt (`sleep 3; hostname` — output not derivable from context). The pinned thread renders the **webchat-origin turn's reply natively** — including the real tool result — through telegram's own dispatch: 1. `/verbose on` → "⚙️ Verbose logging enabled." 2. `/pin on` → **"📌 Pinned. This thread will mirror turns from other threads of this session."** 3. `📱 [via webchat] Use your exec (bash) tool to run … sleep 3; hostname …` 4. The pinned thread receives the real reply: **"The hostname is `crabbox-golden-crayfish-fa445ed1`."** — the actual box hostname, i.e. a real `hostname` exec run by the webchat-triggered turn, rendered natively in the pinned Telegram thread via the channel-agnostic mirror dispatch. Mechanism confirmed in the gateway log for the filmed run: `launchMirrorDispatch` fires for the webchat-origin turn (origin = webchat, telegram target not self-excluded, dispatcher found); the telegram `dispatchMirror` builds the synthetic inbound (`context=BUILT`) and runs `dispatchTelegramMessage` for the full turn (`message processed: channel=telegram messageId=0 … completed`); exactly one reply lands (post-hoc assistant echo skipped by the handled-mark, no duplicate). #### Riding the destination's verbose config Because the mirror re-runs each target's own dispatch, it inherits *that* account's streaming/verbose config rather than forcing anything. With the daily driver's setup (`verboseDefault: on`, `streaming.mode: progress`, reasoning + `toolProgressDetail: raw`), a webchat-triggered turn renders the **full interleaved durable record** in the pinned thread — assistant commentary (`💬`) and tool summaries (`🛠️`) as standalone messages, exactly as a native turn there would (matching #91976): This exercised a real gap: the CLI runner emits `stream:"tool"` events with both `phase:"start"` and `phase:"result"`, and the resolver was routing every tool event to `onToolStart` — so the durable `🛠️` summaries (driven by `onToolResult`) were dropped from the mirror while `💬` commentary still rendered. Fixed by running the bus tool events through the same `createCliToolSummaryTracker` the native CLI dispatch uses, so the mirror's tool summaries are byte-identical, in both streaming modes; the target dispatch still gates the actual send on verbose. ### Public SDK contract The mirror-dispatcher seam (`registerChannelMirrorDispatcher` / `unregisterChannelMirrorDispatcher` / `MirrorDispatcher`) is now **documented** as a public `channel-outbound` contract in `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md` (new *Channel mirror dispatcher* section): per-account registration, the last-wins reload lifecycle, and the account-match + destination-revocation behavior a channel plugin must honor (the native dispatcher is the sole delivery authority, so it must run the channel's own admission/revocation path). The barrel export points at that section. It stays on its neutral SDK subpath per the repo's internal-module convention rather than being reached into as an internal — final acceptance of the public surface is the maintainers' call. ### Tests + CI - `tsgo:core`: clean. Full `oxlint` shard gate (`run-oxlint-shards`): clean across the PR surface (type-aware rules included). - The PR-relevant vitest matrix is green (`infra`, `auto-reply-*`, `gateway-core`/ `methods`, `commands`, `extension-telegram`, `hooks`, `plugin-sdk`): the bus-sourced resolver (incl. the tool-summary replay), handled-mark dedup, and `/pin` fan-out are covered. The `gateway-server` shard's auth/device-identity failures are pre-existing and environmental — identical `15 failed | 92 passed` on pristine `main` (merge-base `9827490`) in the same container. ### AI assistance Built with AI assistance (Claude). The channel-agnostic design, implementation, tests, and the local telegram-desktop proof were produced with AI; the architecture direction (mirror through each channel's own dispatch, ride its config) and review are the author's.

target.title: refactor: add SDK transcript identity target API
target.body:
## What
Adds one combined public SDK transcript runtime API bundle for Path 3. This supersedes #89261 and #89262 by exposing the coherent contract in a single review unit: stable transcript identity plus scoped transcript target/read/write/publish/lock helpers.

## Why
#89261 and #89262 were split across a stale stack, but the public API is one contract. Plugins need identity helpers and scoped target writers together so reads, appends, update publishes, and write locks bind to the same session transcript target without treating `sessionFile` as durable identity. The compatibility behavior remains additive and limited to callers that already receive active transcript artifacts.

## Changes
- Add transcript runtime SDK subpath
- Add transcript memory keys
- Add scoped target/write helpers
- Keep identity reads read-only
- Re-export memory keys from hit subpath
- Update SDK docs and metadata
- Refresh plugin SDK API baseline

## Changes Walkthrough

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-runtime.ts` | Adds public identity, target, read, append, publish, and lock helpers |
| `src/config/sessions/transcript-append.ts` | Adds scoped append transaction helper over existing write lock and FIFO |
| `src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts` | Preserves malformed-line tolerance for parsed transcript reads |
| `src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-hit.ts` | Re-exports transcript memory hit key helpers for compatibility |
| `docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md` | Documents the combined transcript runtime contract |
| `docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md` | Lists the new SDK subpath |
| `package.json` / `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json` | Publish the SDK subpath |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Refreshes the public API baseline hash |

## Verification
- `pnpm docs:list` - passed after workspace deps installed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-runtime.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-hit.test.ts src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts` - passed
- `node scripts/sync-plugin-sdk-exports.mjs --check` - passed
- `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs` - passed
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check` - passed
- `node scripts/check-session-accessor-boundary.mjs` - passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...` - passed
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md` - passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json ...` - passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/sdk-transcript-api-core.tsbuildinfo` - passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/sdk-transcript-api-core-test.tsbuildinfo` - passed
- `git diff --check` - passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --base upstream/main ...` - first run found a read-side mutation bug; fixed and retested
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --base upstream/main ...` - clean

Refs #88838.
Supersedes #89261.
Supersedes #89262.



## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Public SDK transcript runtime contract for Path 3: stable transcript identity plus scoped target/read/write/lock helpers, including deprecated `sessionFile` compatibility for callers that already receive an active transcript artifact.

Real environment tested: Local live OpenClaw gateway from `/Users/phaedrus/Projects/clawdbot`, PR head `08af39905b6bc45caf7ea03a761ebe5b719230f0`, `OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (08af399)`, LaunchAgent on `127.0.0.1:18789`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-runtime.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-hit.test.ts
node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs
node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md
git diff --check
cd /Users/phaedrus/Projects/clawdbot
git fetch upstream clawdbot-d02.1.9.1.20-24/31b-sdk-transcript-identity-target-api
git switch --detach 08af39905b6bc45caf7ea03a761ebe5b719230f0
pnpm build
pnpm openclaw gateway restart
pnpm openclaw --version
pnpm openclaw gateway status --json --require-rpc
pnpm openclaw gateway health --json
pnpm openclaw gateway call health --json
curl -fsS -i http://127.0.0.1:18789/healthz
curl -fsS -i http://127.0.0.1:18789/readyz
node --input-type=module <<'NODE'
import fs from 'node:fs';
import os from 'node:os';
import path from 'node:path';
import {
  appendSessionTranscriptMessageByIdentity,
  readSessionTranscriptEvents,
  resolveSessionTranscriptIdentity,
  resolveSessionTranscriptTarget,
  withSessionTranscriptWriteLock,
} from 'openclaw/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-runtime';
import { upsertSessionEntry } from 'openclaw/plugin-sdk/session-store-runtime';
// Creates a temp sessions.json + active transcript JSONL, resolves identity,
// reads malformed-tolerant events, appends by identity, appends under lock,
// verifies persisted bytes, then removes the temp root.
NODE
```

Evidence after fix: `pnpm openclaw --version` returned `OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (08af399)`. `gateway status --json --require-rpc` reported LaunchAgent `running`, pid `70637`, `rpc.ok: true`, server version `2026.6.8`, and no plugin version drift. `/healthz` returned HTTP 200 with `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`. `/readyz` returned HTTP 200 with `{"ready":true,"failing":[]}`. The public SDK import proof returned `importSurface: "openclaw/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-runtime"`, `identityReturnedSessionFile: false`, `identityReadPersistedSessionFile: false`, `targetReturnedSessionFile: false`, `malformedLineSkipped: true`, `eventsBeforeAppendCount: 2`, `eventsAfterCount: 4`, `persistedContainsAppend: true`, `persistedContainsLockedAppend: true`, `finalStoreHasSessionFile: false`, and `cleanupRemoved: true`.

Observed result after fix: The real gateway started on the patched build, health/readiness/RPC were live, the public package export resolved from the built OpenClaw package, identity and target helpers returned public transcript shapes without `sessionFile`, identity-only reads did not persist `sessionFile`, malformed JSONL rows were skipped, scoped append and write-lock helpers wrote to the active transcript target, and the temporary persisted proof state was removed.

What was not tested: No third-party plugin package was installed for this proof, and no broad Crabbox/Testbox matrix was run. Cleanup note: the temp proof state was removed. The local live gateway is currently still running the PR build because restoring this particular live checkout to local `main` (`2026.6.2`) is blocked by OpenClaw's downgrade guard after the proof build wrote config as `2026.6.8`; the service is healthy on the PR SHA.
